title: Govt. committed to reviving Nandi awards, says Cinematography Minister Durgesh
content: He calls upon producers and film personalities to invest in studios, dubbing theatres and recording facilities in the State; Bharat Bhushan takes over as Andhra Pradesh State Film, Television and Theatre Development Corporation

title: Nine dead in Bhatkal estuary, two missing
content: ‘They likely entered the estuary to collect shellfish and did not estimate the force of the current that had increased due to the recent rainfall’

title: New U.S. Green Card rules raise concerns among professionals, students
content: Indian workers and families, including many from Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, await clarity as proposed immigration changes add to uncertainty over long-term residency and employment prospects

title: Quad remains ‘vital’, says Japanese Foreign Minister, rejecting concerns over the grouping’s importance in the Indo-Pacific
content: Ahead of Quad Foreign Ministers’ Meeting, Toshimitsu Motegi says critical mineral cooperation is at the top of the agenda, calls for better infrastructure, protection for intellectual property rights in India

title: Social media trends echo emotional distress among youth, says TDP leader Palla Srinivasa Rao
content: Referring to the viral ‘Cockroach Janata Party’ movement, he cautions against misuse of social media platforms, but insists that the NDA governments remain committed to addressing aspirations of youth while safeguarding national interests

title: 'Compromised' PM appeasing 'good friend': Congress on Rubio's India trade remarks
content: India's current yearly imports are $52.9 billion as of FY26, and Rubio's statement implies that India will have to double its annual imports from the U.S., highlights Congress leader Jairam Ramesh

title: Vulnerable areas to be mapped in Wayanad ahead of monsoon
content: Officials instructed to identify dangerous drainage points within local body limits and take measures to avert potential hazards ahead of the monsoon season

title: Academic focus, live surgeries feature at MYSOGUS UROCON 2026
content: The annual urology workshop on the theme of urinary incontinence draws specialists from across the country

title: Yatnal trying to influence ECI SIR in his favour, claim Congress leaders
content: Vijayapura district administration urged take to serious note of it and initiate action against the MLA and his staff involved in such an act

title: Protesters seek withdrawal of cases booked during LDF govt.’s tenure
content: Demand, raised by social and religious groups involved in the Sabarimala and Vizhinjam protests, gains momentum with the State government announcing its decision to withdraw cases booked in connection with protests against the now-scrapped semi-high-speed rail corridor project

title: Mann under fire over ASI murder, Kapurthala jail violence, opposition demands resignation
content: Leaders from the Congress, the BJP and the Shiromani Akali Dal targeted the Bhagwant Mann government over the law and order situation, claiming that nobody was safe.

title: Delivery agent’s kindness, constable’s persistence help find missing dog
content: Bruno, a four-year-old Labrador from Sainikpuri, was reunited with his owner after nearly 24 hours of of anxiety and frantic searching

title: Allegations of substandard construction resurface at Pathanamthitta General Hospital
content: Engineer admits at HMC meeting that construction was rushed under political pressure ahead of Assembly elections

title: Falta repoll results highlights: BJP’s Debangshu Panda wins Falta Assembly seat by over 1 lakh votes
content: Panda secured 1,49,666 votes, while CPI(M)’s Sambhu Nath Kurmi came second with 40,645 votes cast in his favour. Congress candidate Abdur Razzak Molla stood third with 10,084 votes

title: Over 60% attendance in UPSC Civil Services prelims at 17 Visakhapatnam centres
content: Of 7,763 registered candidates, 61.25% sat the forenoon paper and 60.49% the afternoon paper across 17 centres; officials report no untoward incident

title: Experts call for urgent action to save endangered Egyptian Vultures in Rayalaseema
content: In the dry scrub forests and rugged cliffs near Ratnagiri Fort in Rolla Mandal of Sri Sathya Sai District, close to the Karnataka border, researchers fear the species may be fighting its last battle for survival in the region

title: Twisha Sharma cremated in Bhopal after 12 days of her death and second autopsy
content: The police have registered an FIR against Twisha’s husband, Samarth Singh, who is a lawyer, and her mother-in-law and former district judge Giribala Singh on charges of harassment for dowry

title: Kerala’s new UDF government proposes measures to improve tax collection, planning and project implementation
content: Proposals part of Vision 2031 roadmap cleared by State government. Revamping Goods and Services Tax department among plans

title: Rozgar Mela: Union Minister Suresh Gopi urges new recruits to collectively realise the vision of ‘Viksit Bharat’
content: As many as 130 candidates receive appointment letters during mela held in Thiruvananthapuram; Minister hails the initiative, says it is part of a larger national vision aimed at transforming India’s youth power into national power

title: BJP’s Debangshu Panda wins Falta Assembly seat by over 1 lakh votes
content: Mr. Panda secured 1,49,666 votes, while CPI(M)’s Sambhu Nath Kurmi came second with 40,645 votes cast in his favour. Congress candidate Abdur Razzak Molla stood third with 10,084 votes

title: Agriculture sector slipping into a crisis in Andhra Pradesh, says YSRCP leader
content: Farmers have lost nearly ₹7,900 crore in the last two years due to falling prices, rising input costs and inadequate government support, alleges Nagi Reddy

title: GI tag application filed for ‘Ananthapuramu Lemon’ from Anantapur district
content: Madanapalle Institute of Technology and Science files the application through its Intellectual Property Facilitation Centre, citing the fruit’s uniform size, bright colour, high juice content and long shelf life

title: NEET paper leak case: Court sends accused Shubham Khairnar to judicial custody till June 6
content: The agency said that judicial custody was also needed to prevent the accused from absconding, destroying physical or digital evidence, and engaging in "further commission of similar offences involving leakage of questions”

title: Marco Rubio in India LIVE: India-U.S. relations 'have not lost momentum'; hope to seal trade deal soon, says Rubio
content: U.S. Secretary of State, who held a meeting with Jaishankar, described the first day of his visit as "fantastic"

title: Hyderabad police aim to break language barriers with AI
content: An AI-powered multilingual complaint recording tool launched by Hyderabad police that can transcribe, translate and convert spoken complaints in 10 Indian languages into FIR-ready documents in real time

title: Nara Lokesh’s Kadapa visit lifts TDP cadre; Reddy community eyes Rajya Sabha seat
content: The working president’s first trip to the former Congress and YSRCP bastion has enthused the party, and Reddy leaders hope the four Rajya Sabha vacancies will finally bring them representation

title: Kerala’s iconic wild elephant ‘Chillikomban’ dies after being washed away in Parambikulam Aliyar Project contour canal
content: Chillikomban was known as a docile tusker that lived amongst people in the Nelliampathi region, without causing trouble. The tusker would traverse to the Tamil Nadu side during musth and return to the home turf, recalled a biologist from Kerala.

title: K.C. Venugopal slams Centre, says no government to address people’s issues in the country
content: AICC general secretary warns of rising youth anger, says unemployment and price rise remain unaddressed

title: Steps will be taken to modernise prison administration: T.N. Law Minister Nirmalkumar
content: Many remand prisoners continue to remain in prison beyond the stipulated period, and the Law Department would explore the possibilities of extending legal aid to them, he said.

title: Ladakh L-G Saxena launches ecological restoration project to reclaim 800 acres of barren land
content: Lieutenant-Governor Vinai Kumar Saxena initiates an ecological restoration project to reclaim 800 acres of barren land in Ladakh

title: Congress factions in Kerala ‘wishcasting’ for their respective loyalists as KPCC president’s post comes up for grabs
content: A Congress insider says AICC seems resolved to prevent current jockeying for KPCC chief’s post from backsliding into a self-destructive circular firing squad at a crucial time when ‘Team-UDF’ seeks to cast LDF, still smarting from its historic defeat, as the alliance in disarray

title: Falta constituency results 2026: Why did the West Bengal seat go to polls again?
content: Voting for the Falta Assembly constituency seat was originally held on April 29 2026, as part of the second phase of the West Bengal Assembly elections.

title: Kerala seeks urgent intervention over CBSE Plus Two evaluation complaints
content: Complaints over evaluation of CBSE Class XII examination papers under newly introduced OSM system, in which answer sheets are scanned and digitally evaluated instead of being physically corrected by teachers

title: Arms and explosives recovered in Chhattisgarh’s Kanker district
content: During the search, the forces recovered a concealed Naxal dump containing a country-made BGL launcher, 25 BGL shells, four improvised fire cartridges, empty cartridges of AK-47 and SLR rifles, around five kilograms of gunpowder

title: T.N. Speaker Prabhakar says decision on AIADMK split will show his neutrality
content: Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar paid tributes to former Speaker Si.Pa. Adithanar in Chennai on the late leader’s death anniversary on May 24, 2026

title: Trump turnaround: Open the straits, close the war
content: A new understanding with Iran is emerging to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. This comes after weeks of military escalation. The United States seeks to prevent global economic disruption from energy shipment issues. An agreement is reportedly nearing finalization, though details remain unclear. Iran denies commitments to give up uranium or unconditionally reopen the straits.

title: Loans, PG rent & coaching fees: The crushing cost of NEET-UG 2026
content: NEET-UG 2026 exam cancellation due to paper leak has deeply affected lakhs of students. Families face financial strain and students endure immense mental pressure. This incident raises serious questions about the integrity of competitive examinations in India. The system's credibility is shaken, while also impacting the aspirations of countless young individuals.

title: Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: Watch: Hypersonic Oreshnik strikes in blinding flash as Russia unleashes massive barrage on Kyiv
content: Kyiv endured a massive overnight aerial assault, with Russia deploying over 700 drones and 50 missiles, including the hypersonic Oreshnik. The barrage, described as one of the war's most intense, struck government buildings and residential areas, causing casualties and widespread damage. Russia stated the strikes targeted military facilities in retaliation for Ukrainian attacks.

title: Iran secretly bought Chinese satellite tech via UAE — then struck Abu Dhabi: Report
content: Leaked records reveal Iran's Revolutionary Guards secretly acquired advanced Chinese satellite communication equipment via a UAE-based procurement network. The technology, crucial for Tehran's drone and missile programs, was shipped through Dubai, highlighting sophisticated methods to bypass Western sanctions and reach Iran's military apparatus.

title: 'Going to be beneficial to both': Rubio on 'sustainable' India-US trade deal
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated a "beneficial" and "sustainable" trade deal with India is likely, following significant progress. He emphasized that the Trump administration's trade policy aims to address global imbalances, not target specific nations. Rubio's visit also included discussions on critical minerals, energy, and defense, while India raised concerns about US visa policies.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: Jadeja mocks Shardul Thakur in wild IPL moment after dramatic no-ball twist
content: A dramatic final over saw Ravindra Jadeja turn the tables on former CSK teammate Shardul Thakur. After Thakur thought he had Jadeja caught, a no-ball overturned the dismissal. Jadeja then unleashed two boundaries on the free-hit and subsequent ball, adding a playful taunt that delighted fans and spiced up the tense IPL clash.

title: Spain’s housing crisis: Thousands hit Madrid streets to protest rising rents and home prices
content: Thousands protested in Madrid against soaring housing costs, a major challenge for Prime Minister Sanchez. Spain's homeownership tradition clashes with limited public rentals and rising demand from tourism and population growth. Despite a 7 billion euro plan for public housing, a rent freeze extension failed, intensifying criticism over the ongoing housing crisis.

title: Why OpenAI is paying $445,000 for a 'tasteful and strategic' AI safety researcher
content: OpenAI is seeking a highly paid researcher to explore the potential of AI that can improve itself, a concept moving beyond theory. This role on the Preparedness team emphasizes strategic judgment alongside technical skill, as the individual will model future risks and track AI's growing role in several technical tasks.

title: Stock market watch: Middle East tensions, oil prices & more to guide Dalal Street
content: Dalal Street braces for a week of global uncertainty, with Middle East tensions, crude oil prices, and foreign investor behavior dominating market sentiment. Hopes for US-Iran negotiation progress offer a glimmer of optimism, but caution prevails. Investors will also monitor the rupee's movement and the RBI's significant dividend payout.

title: Wankhede goes numb as Rohit Sharma falls for duck in MI' last match
content: Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Mumbai Indians' Rohit Sharma both failed to deliver in a crucial match. Despite early top-order struggles, Rajasthan Royals posted 205 for eight, propelled by late fireworks from Jofra Archer and Ravindra Jadeja.

title: Mumbai Indians bring back rare 11-year-old strategy vs Rajasthan Royals
content: Mumbai Indians tried a new tactic in their final league match. They used three overs of spin in the Powerplay against Rajasthan Royals. This unusual strategy was also seen against Lucknow Super Giants earlier in IPL 2026. It is a rare move for the team in their IPL history. Mumbai Indians have had a mixed season in IPL 2026.

title: Emotional scenes as family performs last rites of Twisha Sharma 12 days after her death – video
content: Twisha Sharma's funeral was held in Bhopal as her family continues to demand justice in the alleged dowry death case. The Supreme Court has taken suo motu cognisance of the matter, citing institutional bias, while a second post-mortem is being conducted. Her husband, the prime accused, has been arrested and remanded to police custody.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Chargers, Dolphins eye Steelers’ $4m QB
content: Mason Rudolph is emerging as a potential trade candidate for the Pittsburgh Steelers after reportedly falling to QB3 during offseason workouts behind Aaron Rodgers and rookie Will Howard. Teams like the Los Angeles Chargers, Jacksonville Jaguars, Miami Dolphins, and Denver Broncos have been linked as possible destinations for the veteran backup quarterback.

title: ‘Feared Ladakh would turn into another Manipur’: Wangchuk says trust deficit with Centre remains
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk acknowledged a positive step in talks between Ladakh representatives and the Centre, but warned trust-building remains incomplete. He cited unresolved protest cases and institutional issues, fearing Ladakh could become "another Manipur" due to growing unrest. Wangchuk stressed that government intent will be judged by actions on these outstanding matters.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi flop show triggers viral Hardik Pandya reaction
content: Deepak Chahar provided Mumbai Indians with a crucial breakthrough, dismissing Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi early in their IPL 2026 clash at Wankhede.  Sooryavanshi's dismissal, caught by Naman Dheer, sent the crowd into a frenzy. Mumbai Indians opted to bowl first in this must-win encounter for Rajasthan Royals.

title: Watch: Childhood friends turn rivals, ram vehicles in violent road rage over woman in Vijayawada
content: Childhood friends and distant relatives, Lingamaneni Rohit and Bolla Om, engaged in a violent car confrontation in Vijayawada over a woman. The dispute escalated from WhatsApp messages to ramming vehicles and using weapons, causing panic. Police took both into custody, suspecting ganja influence, with initial tests showing Om positive.

title: 'Mungerilal ke haseen sapne': CM Fadnavis mocks Rahul's prediction of NDA's fall
content: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis dismissed Rahul Gandhi's prediction of the NDA government's collapse as "daydreaming," comparing it to a popular television serial. Fadnavis made these remarks while inaugurating a logistics park in Nagpur, highlighting its potential to boost the manufacturing sector and strengthen the city's position as a logistics hub.

title: When and where will banks remain closed this week? Check state-wise list
content: Bank customers across India should note extended holiday closures from May 26-28, 2026, impacting numerous states for national and religious observances like Kazi Nazrul Islam's birth anniversary and Eid-ul-Adha.  Remember, second and fourth Saturdays also mean branch shutdowns. Plan ahead for essential services, as digital banking remains fully operational.

title: Karnataka river tragedy: 7 women among 8 dead in Bhatkal mishap
content: Tragedy struck Karnataka's Uttara Kannada district on Sunday as at least eight individuals, predominantly women, lost their lives after being swept away while bathing in the Thatte Hakkalu river near Bhatkal. A massive search and rescue operation is underway, with authorities concerned the grim toll may escalate. Further updates are anticipated as the situation unfolds.

title: Kangana Ranaut pens heartfelt note for veteran actress Vyjayanthimala
content: Kangana Ranaut paid a heartfelt tribute to legendary actress and dancer Vyjayanthimala on social media, sharing a vintage Bharatanatyam video and calling her "heavenly" and "born only to be worshipped." Vyjayanthimala, a Padma Vibhushan awardee, is celebrated for iconic films like 'Nagin', 'Devdas', and her legendary pairing with Dilip Kumar.

title: NFL insiders predict troubling end to Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini controversy
content: The controversy involving Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini continues to dominate NFL headlines, with analysts split on how it will end. Many believe the scandal will fade if the New England Patriots keep winning, while others think Russini’s possible public response could reignite the story. Several insiders warned the situation may permanently affect Vrabel’s reputation and place extra pressure on New England heading into the 2026 season.

title: From anti-India racism to visa issues - Key takeaways from Rubio-Jaishankar joint press conference
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and external affairs minister S Jaishankar reaffirmed strong US-India strategic ties, highlighting cooperation in defence, trade, energy and technology. Rubio said US visa reforms were global, not India-specific, while condemning racist remarks against Indian Americans. Both leaders discussed energy security, Quad cooperation, trade negotiations, Iran tensions and India’s multi-alignment foreign policy approach.

title: Indiramma housing scheme phase 2: Telangana approves construction of 2.5 lakh houses
content: The state of Telangana is making a significant leap in housing infrastructure by initiating the second phase of the Indiramma scheme, targeting the establishment of 250,000 homes to replace dilapidated kutcha houses. Additionally, the government is stepping in to support ongoing constructions, with financial assistance reaching up to Rs 3 lakh for structures still at the foundation phase.

title: Indo-US defence ties: Jaishankar pushes ‘Make in India’ approach in talks with Rubio
content: India and the US are prioritizing 'Make in India' in defense cooperation, following recent talks between external affairs minister S Jaishankar and US secretary of State Marco Rubio. Discussions also covered energy security, trade, and advancements in critical technologies like semiconductors and AI, underscoring a deepening strategic alliance.

title: Amid heatwave, tourists rush to hills; Manali sees severe traffic congestion
content: Manali experienced severe traffic congestion late Saturday night due to a surge in tourist arrivals. Long vehicle queues formed across the hill town, leaving many commuters and tourists stranded for hours. The slow traffic movement, attributed to the weekend rush, caused significant inconvenience.

title: Blogger held for 'using Shiv idol reel to promote eatery'
content: A 25-year-old food influencer, Anas Ahmed, found himself in hot water in Muzaffarnagar after right-wing activists lodged a complaint. They claimed that a short promotional video for a local eatery, which featured imagery of a Shiv idol, was offensive to religious sentiments.

title: ‘Didn’t give money for drugs’: Woman, teen son stabbed to death in Delhi
content: A shocking double murder in Govindpuri, Delhi, has led to the arrest of the victim's cousin. Police apprehended Saurabh Sahu after a brief shootout. He allegedly confessed to killing his cousin and her son due to drug addiction and resentment over financial matters. The teen was killed trying to intervene.

title: 24 killed as explosives-laden car rams into train carrying Pakistan military personnel
content: At least 24 Pakistani army personnel were killed and more than 100 injured after a suicide vehicle bomb targeted a military shuttle train in Quetta, Balochistan, on Sunday. The blast derailed a bogie, sparked a fire and damaged nearby homes and vehicles. The banned Baloch Liberation Army’s Majeed Brigade claimed responsibility, saying army personnel were the intended target.

title: 'Thank the court': BJP leader welcomes HC relief for Vinesh Phogat in WFI dispute
content: The Delhi high court’s order came after the Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) had declared Vinesh ineligible for domestic events till June 26. Babita, herself a former wrestler, said the court’s decision was correct.

title: Cockroach Janata Party founder’s house in Maharashtra gets police protection
content: Police protection has been deployed outside the residence of Abhijeet Dipke in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar after the online rise of the Cockroach Janata Party. Police said the move was precautionary due to growing public attention, not threats. The group recently gained popularity through memes and commentary on unemployment, exam paper leaks and education issues. Dipke also alleged its social media accounts and website were compromised.

title: 'We will not stop': Rahul vows more NEET protests until education minister resigns
content: Rahul Gandhi demands Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-UG 2026 controversy, vowing continued protests until a foolproof system prevents future paper leaks. The NSUI also called for the minister's resignation and an impartial investigation, as the NTA claims only some questions, not the full paper, were leaked.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 67: Can Ranveer Singh film net Rs 1150 crore this weekend?
content: Aditya Dhar's historic spy thriller 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', starring Ranveer Singh, enters its final theatrical stretch on Day 67. With an official JioHotstar domestic OTT release date locked for June 4, 2026, the box office juggernaut is racing to secure its next massive milestone. The film currently sits just Rs 3.65 crore away from crossing the monumental Rs 1150 crore net domestic mark in India. Fans looking forward to the high-octane action can also gear up for its dual-platform digital release next month, which features the unfiltered "Raw & Undekha" cut.

title: 30-year membership wait, ‘green-card’ system: Inside Delhi's elite Gymkhana Club
content: The Centre has ordered the historic Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate its 27.3-acre Lutyens' Delhi premises by June 5, citing urgent need for defence infrastructure and public security. This move, stemming from governance disputes and alleged mismanagement, has shocked members and sparked debate over elite colonial clubs on prime government land.

title: Iran hits back at Rubio’s ‘energy hostage’ remark, blames US sanctions for market turmoil
content: Iran has rejected US Secretary of State Marco Rubio's remarks, accusing Washington of distorting regional realities and diverting attention from its destabilizing policies. Tehran asserts that US sanctions, not its own actions, have disrupted global energy markets and held them hostage. Rubio had previously accused Iran of holding the energy market hostage during his visit to India.

title: Canadian GP: Russell snatches pole as Antonelli, McLaren and Ferrari up for fierce battle
content: George Russell clinched pole for the Canadian Grand Prix, just after winning the Sprint race. His Mercedes teammate, championship leader Andrea Kimi Antonelli, starts second. McLaren and Ferrari are pushing hard, while Red Bull faces challenges. An intense intra-team rivalry between Russell and Antonelli adds further drama to the highly anticipated race.

title: Explained: How Ukraine's Drone Expertise Became Global Asset After Iran War
content: Ukraine’s years of battlefield experience with drone technology and air defence tactics have drawn attention from Gulf nations, European allies, and the United States.

title: Marco Rubio India Visit Live Updates: "India Among Few Nations Able To Influence Global Issues": Marco Rubio
content: Marco Rubio India Visit Live Updates: The top US diplomat is scheduled to hold talks with External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar and attend a meeting of the Quad foreign ministers.

title: BJP Wins Repoll In Bengal's Falta By Over 1 Lakh Votes
content: Repolling was ordered in all 285 booths in Falta after allegations of tampering with the voting machines and electoral irregularities during April 29 voting.

title: Twisha Sharma Death Case Updates: Twisha Sharma's Last Rites Performed In Bhopal
content: Twisha Sharma Death Updates: The case came under scrutiny as the Madhya Pradesh Police faced criticism for an inefficient probe into the case, while Giribala Singh was accused of exerting undue influence.

title: 16 Dead Due To Heatstroke In Telangana, Rs 4 Lakh Compensation Announced
content: Telangana Government has announced Rs 4 lakh compensation each to the families of the deceased who lost their lives due to heatstroke.

title: Andhra Government On "High Alert" Over Ebola Virus, Initiates Screening At Airports
content: Andhra Pradesh Health Minister Satya Kumar Yadav on Sunday said the state government is on high alert and has initiated precautionary measures in view of the spread of the Ebola virus and rising fatalities reported in parts of Africa.

title: "Positive Shift": Sonam Wangchuk As Centre Allows Elected Legislature In Ladakh
content: Sonam Wangchuk described the breakthrough in an exclusive conversation with NDTV as a "very different and positive stand" from the government's previous stance

title: Four Dead In Horrific Bike Collision Near Assam's Guwahati
content: The tragic collision took place around 2 am near Bajrapara under the jurisdiction of Palashbari Police Station.

title: NEET Paper Leak Case Accused Sent To Judicial Custody Till June 6
content: A Delhi court on Sunday sent accused Shubham Khairnar to judicial custody till June 6 in the NEET paper leak case.

title: Assam To Set Up Counter-Radicalisation Cell To Protect Youth: Himanta Sarma
content: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma on Sunday said his government will set up a counter-radicalisation cell to "protect vulnerable youth from extremist influence".

title: 10 Drown While Collecting Mussel Shells In Karnataka River, Several Missing
content: Ten people drowned while venturing into a river to collect mussel shells in Karnataka's Uttara Kannada district

title: Karnataka NEET Aspirant Dies By Suicide, Had Scored 92% In Board Exam
content: According to police, Bhagyashree allegedly died by suicide by hanging herself from a ceiling fan at her apartment.

title: Bengal To Set Up 'Holding Centres' In Every District To Keep Illegal Immigrants
content: These holding centres will come up in every district to house people who get detained under suspicion of being Bangladeshi national or Rohingya

title: Terror Hideout Busted In J&K's Baramulla, Ammunition Recovered
content: Sources said the timely recovery has averted a potentially grave security threat and marked a major success in the sustained anti-terror drive across north Kashmir.

title: Road Blocked Over Missing People, IAF Airlifts NCC Cadets From Imphal
content: With road connectivity affected, due to blockade and counter blockade by Kuki and Naga groups, the airlift was the only option left.

title: Top US Diplomat Marco Rubio Was Asked About Racism Against Indians. His Reply
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who is on a four-day visit to India, stressed that the US is a very welcoming country.

title: The final ‘metamorphosis’: Can cockroaches win against humans in real world?
content: cockroaches have been disliked and despised by humans since ages . but today, they are being “liked” and “followed” like never before . they would never have imagined, even in their wildest dreams, that one day would become the centre of a human movement .
